title: Customer Service Representative (CSR) – HVAC, Plumbing & Electrical Company
description:
We are seeking a highly organized, detail-oriented, and customer-focused Virtual Customer Service Representative (CSR) to join our fast-growing home services team. This role is responsible for delivering exceptional service to our customers while supporting daily operations for our HVAC, plumbing, and electrical divisions. The ideal candidate is tech-savvy, proactive, empathetic, and able to thrive in a fast-paced environment.



Key Responsibilities

1. Inbound Call Handling



-Answer incoming calls promptly and professionally while following guided call prompts in ServiceTitan.



-Greet customers with warmth, empathy, and professionalism.



-Use active listening to fully understand customer concerns and service requests.



-Ask qualifying questions regarding service type, urgency, location, and referral source.



-Confirm the customer’s issue falls within our service offerings and service area.



2. Service Qualification & Intake



-Collect and confirm essential customer details: name, address, phone,
, and preferred contact method.



-Identify the type of service needed (AC repair, plumbing leak, drain clog, electrical panel issue, etc.).



-Determine urgency level (emergency, routine, or maintenance).



-Check customer profiles for service history, membership status, warranty info, and important account notes.



-Verify whether the issue is related to a previous job or system we serviced.



3. Pricing & Service Information



-Provide preliminary estimates for common diagnostics and services (e.g., $99 diagnostic fee).



-Set expectations for the service visit, including technician process and upfront pricing after diagnosis.



-Inform customers about current promotions, rebates, memberships, and savings opportunities.



4. Scheduling & Dispatch Coordination



-Book appointments according to availability using ServiceTitan scheduling modules.



-Offer scheduling options based on the 3-day call board, same-day availability (if down), or estimate availability.



-Coordinate with dispatch for same-day bookings or priority situations.



-Communicate cancellation policies and provide accurate service expectations.



5. Outbound Calls & Follow-Up



-Return missed calls and respond to voi
s within 30 minutes (1 hour max).



-Confirm, reschedule, or follow up on upcoming appointments.



-Conduct outbound campaigns for:



-Tune-ups



-Plumbing inspections



-Electrical inspections



-Meet minimum outbound calling goals set by management.



-Contact customers on “hold” status and bring them back on the schedule.



-Create and verify outbound calling lists.



6. Home Depot Lead Management



-Accept and process incoming Home Depot leads inside ServiceTitan’s booking tab.



-Enter leads into tracking spreadsheets and create profiles ensuring the F-number and tags are accurate.



-Immediately call the lead—if no answer, leave voi
 and send a text.



-Update lead tracking sheets and the Home Depot Service Center with correct outcomes.



-Schedule jobs for customers who convert and ensure both systems are updated.



-Complete a structured follow-up process (Day 2, 4, 6, 7) before dismissing unresponsive leads.



-Record every contact attempt in both the tracker and Home Depot Service Center.



7. CRM & Documentation



-Log all customer interactions accurately in ServiceTitan and/or Slack.



-Maintain up-to-date records including service notes, history, equipment, and contact information.



-Tag opportunities, flag priority issues, and escalate leads for management review.



8. Customer Issue Resolution



-Address customer concerns, service delays, or complaints with empathy and professionalism.



-Document issues and updates in Slack and ServiceTitan.



-Escalate concerns when needed to managers or service leadership.



-Track ongoing issues and provide customers with timely follow-ups.



9. Internal Communication



-Communicate job updates, cancellations, urgent customer needs, or scheduling issues to dispatch and office staff in Slack.



-Participate in morning huddles or team briefings when required.



-Share technical issues, customer feedback, or system concerns promptly with management.



10. General Administrative Support



-Assist with
ing invoices, estimates, receipts, or documentation to customers.



-Monitor and respond to support inbox inquiries (Yelp, Thumbtack, Chiirp, Contractor Commerce, FPL, BBB, Brita Pro, and others) within 30 minutes–1 hour.



-Support marketing campaigns by completing outbound call lists or follow-up tasks.



-Assist in managing customer reviews (Google,
, etc.) and reputation initiatives.



11. End-of-Day Wrap-Up



-Review daily call logs and ensure all documentation is complete and accurate.



-Flag unresolved issues or follow-ups for next-day attention.



-Communicate urgent matters to the on-call or after-hours team.



12. Data Entry & System Updates (ServiceTitan)



-Add and update customer equipment details (brand, model, serial number, age).



-Apply appropriate job, customer, and location tags throughout the CRM.



-Ensure all data is accurate, consistent, and properly categorized.



Qualifications



-Previous experience in customer service, HVAC/plumbing/electrical scheduling, or call center preferred.



-Strong communication and active listening skills.



-Ability to remain calm and empathetic in high-volume or emergency situations.



-Proficiency with CRM software (ServiceTitan preferred but not required).



-Strong multitasking and organizational abilities.



-Reliable internet connection, quiet workspace, and ability to work remote full-time.



-Detail-oriented and comfortable following structured workflows and call scripts.



To get started with our interview process please follow the link below!

title: SEO Specialist
description:
Role:

This is a SEO role focused on growing our own company's organic visibility. You'll own our search presence end-to-end — from technical audits to content strategy — with the goal of driving more inbound leads, increasing qualified traffic, and growing the number of discovery calls booked each month.



What You'll Be Responsible For

- Own and grow our organic search rankings for high-intent keywords related to remote staffing, offshore hiring, and talent placement — with a clear monthly target of increasing keyword rankings and driving measurable growth in inbound discovery calls (target: 20–40+ qualified calls/month from organic)



- Conduct regular technical SEO audits and optimizations across the website — including site speed, crawlability, internal linking, schema markup, and on-page structure



- Develop and execute a content strategy aligned with our service offerings, targeting decision-makers (founders, COOs, hiring managers) searching for staffing and outsourcing solutions

Build and manage our local and industry-specific SEO presence, including Google Business Profile, directory listings, and niche staffing/HR platforms to improve local and service-based search visibility



- Track, report, and iterate on core SEO KPIs monthly — including organic sessions, keyword position movements, click-through rates, and call/lead volume attributed to organic search





What We're Looking For

- 3+ years of hands-on SEO experience, with at least 1–2 years in a service-based or B2B industry (staffing, recruiting, HR tech, or professional services strongly preferred)

- Proven track record of improving keyword rankings and driving organic lead generation — not just traffic, but conversions

- Proficiency in SEO tools: Google Search Console, SEMrush or Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, Google Analytics 4

- Strong understanding of both on-page and technical SEO; content strategy experience is a big plus



Nice to Have

- Experience with Webflow or similar CMS

- Familiarity with the staffing, outsourcing, or recruitment industry

- Basic knowledge of CRO (conversion rate optimization) to support lead quality improvements





What Success Looks Like in 90 Days

- Full technical audit completed with priority fixes implemented

-10+ target keywords ranking on page 1 or showing significant upward movement

- Organic-attributed discovery calls increasing month-over-month

- A clear 6-month SEO roadmap presented to the founder





If you're interested, Send the following:

- updated copy of CV

- Video Introduction

- Portfolio or past case studies

title: DevCo — Operations Coordinator
description:
ABOUT THE ROLE



We're a small crypto/fintech development company building multiple products. We need an operations coordinator to run our AI-powered business systems daily, monitor outputs, flag issues, and keep the team informed. 



Think of it like a team captain — you make sure every play runs smoothly, every system delivers its result, and the coach (CEO) always knows the score. The difference is that most of your "players" are AI tools and automated processes. Your job is to trigger them, check their work, and improve them over time. 



This is not a traditional VA role. You will operate AI systems as the core of your job. If that excites you, keep reading.





WHAT YOU'LL DO



Daily operations: 

- Run automated business processes — content generation, invoicing, reporting, audit triggers 

- Check outputs — did the process produce the right result? Is the quality acceptable? 

- Flag issues immediately — "all systems green except these two, which need these inputs" 

- Prepare async updates for the CEO — short video or written briefings with clear agenda items



Communication: 

- Track action items from team calls and follow up on deadlines 

- Communicate with team members and external partners as needed 

- Maintain documentation — when a process changes, the docs change with it 



System improvement: 

-The systems you operate are designed to be iterated. When you notice something isn't working or could be better, you flag it, propose a fix, and help implement the improvement 

- You don't just follow instructions — you learn why each step exists and think about what's missing 

- Over time, you'll suggest new automations and process improvements based on what you observe 





WHAT YOU WON'T DO

- Build the systems from scratch (we have engineers for that) 

-Make strategic business decisions (CEO does that) 

- Manage other people (no direct reports to start)





WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



Must have: 

-You operate AI-powered systems daily — this is core, not optional 

-Strong attention to detail — you catch when an output isn't right 

-You think in steps — when something breaks, you trace back to which step failed and figure out why 

-Clear communicator — written and verbal English fluency. You can explain a problem and a solution in 2 sentences, not 20 

-Proactive — you flag problems before being asked. You prepare updates without being reminded. You don't wait to be told what to do 

-Not afraid to speak up — you raise points, flag issues immediately, and push back when something doesn't make sense 

-Comfortable learning new tools quickly — we'll teach you our specific systems, but you need to be someone who picks things up fast 



Nice to have: 

- Experience with crypto, fintech, or DeFi 

- Familiarity with Discord,
, social media management 

-Previous operations or process management experience 

- Experience working with international remote teams





DETAILS



Remote 

- The team lead is based in Morocco — working hours will need to align accordingly. Flexibility required. Weekends may be needed depending on project sprints. 

- ~20-30 hours/week to start, may grow 

- Starting at $500/month USD 

- 2-week paid trial period (probation) 

- Reports to: CEO + COO (during transition period for system learning) 

- Growth potential: this role can grow into a full operations manager position as the company scales 





HOW TO APPLY



Step 1: Send your CV and a short video (3-5 minutes) to 
 



Video instructions: Record a short video (Loom, phone, any tool). Answer this scenario: 



"Imagine you start your day and you're responsible for checking 4 different automated systems — a report generator, an invoice tool, a content scheduler, and a monitoring alert system. Three ran perfectly overnight. One produced an error. Walk us through: How do you start your day? How do you check each system? When you find the error, what do you do? Who do you tell and how?" 



We're not looking for a perfect answer. We want to see how you think through problems. 



Step 2: If selected, you'll complete a short practical case study (30-60 minutes) 

Step 3: Interview with the team

title: DM Setter – Social Media Inbox & Comment Management
description:
HLH is hiring an experienced DM Setter to manage all inbound direct messages and social media comments across our platforms. You'll be the first point of contact for every person who reaches out, and your number one priority is speed to lead — responding fast, keeping conversations moving, and making sure no opportunity slips through the cracks.

If you've never worked in a DM setting role before, this isn't the position for you. We need someone who already knows the rhythm of high-volume inboxes and understands how to turn a cold message into a booked call.

What You'll Be Doing



Responding to all inbound DMs in real time across platforms (Instagram,
, etc.)

Engaging with comments to spark conversations and move prospects into DMs

Following scripts and SOPs to qualify leads and set appointments

Maintaining the fastest possible response times — speed to lead is the metric we care about most

Tracking conversations and updating CRM or lead tracking tools

Flagging urgent or high-priority messages for the team

Reporting on response times, conversation volume, and outcomes



What We're Looking For



Proven experience as a DM setter or in a similar appointment-setting role

A track record of fast response times and high-volume message handling

Strong written English — clear, conversational, and persuasive

Comfortable working US hours (Eastern, Central, or Pacific time zone schedules)

Self-starter who stays on top of notifications without being told

Familiarity with CRM tools, social media platforms, and lead management workflows



How to Apply

In your application, please include:



Where you worked — company or client name and industry

What you were responsible for — specific DM setting duties, volume handled, and results achieved

What tech tools you used — platforms, CRMs, scheduling tools, automation software, etc.



Applications without this information will not be considered.

title: Wound Care Billing Expert
description:
We are seeking a highly experienced medical billing specialist with deep expertise in wound care billing to support and optimize our revenue cycle. This is not a general billing role — we are specifically looking for someone who understands the clinical, coding, and reimbursement nuances of wound care services.



Our organization works with providers delivering advanced wound care treatments, and we need a professional who can ensure maximum reimbursement, compliance, and clean claim submission.

 We are seeking an experienced wound care billing specialist with deep expertise in Medicare Part B and commercial insurance billing for advanced wound care services. This role goes beyond basic billing—we need someone who understands the nuances of coding and reimbursement for debridement, skin substitutes (Q-codes/CTPs), NPWT, and E/M optimization, including proper modifier usage and documentation requirements for medical necessity. Responsibilities include claim submission, denial management, AR follow-up, and identifying opportunities to improve reimbursement and reduce inefficiencies. The ideal candidate has 3+ years of direct wound care billing experience, strong knowledge of CMS guidelines and LCD policies, and a proven track record of increasing collections and reducing denials. CPC or similar certification is preferred. Please include a brief summary of your relevant experience, examples of results achieved, and your rate.

title: Part-Time WooCommerce Developer
description:
Part-Time WooCommerce Developer — StreamSmile™

Company: StreamSmile™

Location: Fully Remote (Anywhere)

Type: Part-Time / As Needed

Rate: $5/hr

Start Date: Immediate



About StreamSmile™

StreamSmile™ is a fast-growing e-commerce brand specializing in premium portable smart displays — including portable TVs, digital calendars, and digital photo frames. We are a direct-to-consumer brand scaling rapidly and are currently migrating our store from Shopify to WooCommerce.



About the Role

We are looking for a reliable, experienced part-time WordPress/WooCommerce developer to join our team on a flexible, as-needed basis. You will work directly with the founder to help maintain and improve our WooCommerce store, make updates to product pages, build and test sales funnels, and ensure our store is always running smoothly.

This is a great long-term opportunity for someone who wants consistent part-time work with a growing e-commerce brand.



What You'll Be Working On



Making updates and changes to our WooCommerce store and product pages

Building and testing sales funnels using FunnelKit or similar WooCommerce plugins

Installing, configuring, and troubleshooting WordPress plugins

Setting up and adjusting page layouts using Elementor or similar page builders

A/B testing funnels and product pages to improve conversions

General WordPress maintenance and support

Implementing design changes as directed by the founder

Troubleshooting any store issues quickly and efficiently





What We're Looking For

Required:



Minimum 2 years of hands-on WordPress and WooCommerce experience

Strong understanding of WooCommerce product pages, checkout flows, and settings

Experience with page builders such as Elementor or Divi

Experience with funnel plugins (FunnelKit, CartFlows, or similar)

Ability to troubleshoot and resolve issues independently

Reliable, responsive, and communicative

Comfortable working async and managing your own time



Nice to Have:



Previous experience working with Shopify (helpful for understanding our background)

Experience with conversion rate optimization (CRO)

Experience with WooCommerce payment gateway integrations





What to Expect



Flexible hours — work as needed, no fixed schedule

Tasks will be assigned as they come up — some weeks may be more than others

Direct communication with the founder

Long-term opportunity as the brand grows

Opportunity to take on more hours and responsibility over time





How to Apply



A brief introduction about yourself and your experience

Examples of WooCommerce stores you have built or worked on (links preferred)

Confirmation that you are comfortable with the $5/hr rate



We look forward to hearing from you!



StreamSmile™ is an equal opportunity employer. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and locations.ShareProject contentStreamSmile Created by you. Add PDFs, documents, or other text to reference in this project. Content

title: Copywriter & Creative Strategist
description:
Overview

We are seeking a "pioneer"—a proactive, high-energy Copywriter & Creative Strategist to join an experimental, high-conviction project for 2026. This is not a traditional retainer-based role; it is a pure commission-based affiliate model designed to empower our creators and scale our brands through organic social lift. 

In this role, you will act as the "owner" of your own AI-driven Instagram presence, providing the creative vision and briefs while our elite internal production studio handles the AI generation for you—free of charge.



Work Structure & Compensation

Type: Part-time / Commission-based (Affiliate).

Compensation: 10%–50% commission per sale (e.g., a 50% commission is $50 on a $100 product).

Transparency: Commission rates are tiered and will change over time as the project scales.

Growth Opportunity: The highest-performing candidates will be fast-tracked into full-time retainer roles with the company.

Work Requirement: You may have a concurrent full-time job; however, all candidates must sign an NDA



Key Responsibilities

Creative Briefing: Develop winning formats such as AI-driven podcasts, TED talks, or street interviews to sell products to a health-conscious 45+ demographic.

Production Partnership: Brief our internal AI production studio to create high-fidelity, realistic B-roll and AI-influencer content.

Account Management: Run and grow your own Instagram accounts dedicated to promoting the brand.

Performance Testing: Experiment with different formats and AI personas to find the highest-converting proof of concept.

Scaling Strategy: Identify "winner" content and whitelist these assets with our paid ads to "put gasoline on the fire" and scale results.

Strategic Iteration: Use organic performance data to refine hooks, calls-to-action (CTAs), and storytelling strategies. 



Required Skills & Experience

Direct Response Copywriting: Proven ability to write clear, compelling, and high-converting scripts and briefs focused on driving immediate action.

Strategic Thinking: Deep understanding of marketing fundamentals and consumer psychology (e.g., Eugene Schwartz’s Breakthrough Advertising) to create "thumb-stopping" social media content.

AI Awareness: Comfortable working within AI-driven workflows; if not currently comfortable, you must be highly willing to learn and adapt quickly.

Market Knowledge: Understanding of the "TikTok Shop" model and how to apply it to
 and Instagram.

Mindset: A true team player who is communicative, proactive, and maintains a "team-first" attitude while operating with a pioneer spirit.



Why Join Us?

Resource Support: We will work to provide as many resources as possible to ensure your success, including free access to our world-class production studio.

Faceless Commission: A unique, faceless way to earn significant commissions by running your own AI influencer pages.

Direct Impact: We operate on a "real relationship" basis—we don’t make money unless you make money.



To Apply

Please submit your portfolio or examples of previous creative strategies. Include a short Loom video (2–3 minutes) introducing yourself and explaining why you are the right person to pioneer this AI-affiliate model.

title: Sales & Booking Specialist
description:
***To proceed with the application process, please upload your resume into this Google form:
 and select the role.***



Role Summary

As a Sales & Booking Specialist, you are responsible for converting guest inquiries into confirmed bookings across Airbnb, VRBO, Zillow, and direct channels.



Your core objective is to maximize conversion rate, occupancy, and revenue through fast response, strong sales communication, and effective follow-up.



This is a performance-driven sales role, not just customer service. You are expected to actively guide guests through the booking process and drive results.



Work Schedule

 Standard: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM Pacific Time

 Flexibility required: occasional shifts in Philippines Standard Time based on business needs



Key Responsibilities

1. Lead Conversion & Sales



 Respond to guest inquiries across Airbnb, VRBO, Zillow, and other platforms in a timely manner

 Identify guest needs and recommend suitable properties

 Use persuasive communication to highlight property features, amenities, and location advantages

 Address objections and remove booking friction

 Proactively follow up with leads to maximize conversion



2. Revenue & Listing Performance



 Take ownership of booking performance for assigned listings

 Monitor key metrics:

 Traffic

 Inquiry volume

 Conversion rate

 Occupancy



 Identify underperforming listings and recommend improvements (pricing, photos, positioning)

 Collaborate with marketing and pricing teams on optimization strategies



3. Booking Management & Guest Coordination



 Guide guests through the booking process and ensure all information is clearly communicated

 Coordinate with internal teams (operations, cleaning, maintenance) to ensure readiness

 Maintain high response rate and fast response time

 Assist with booking modifications and guest coordination



4. Performance Tracking & Reporting



 Track and report:

 Lead volume

 Inquiry-to-booking conversion rate

 Occupancy rate

 Revenue per listing



 Provide insights and recommendations to improve booking performance



Description



Month2Month is a fast-growing hospitality and property management company focused on maximizing occupancy and delivering exceptional guest experiences.

As a  Sales & Booking Specialist, you will play a critical role in driving revenue by converting guest inquiries into confirmed bookings across multiple platforms including Airbnb, VRBO, and Zillow.

This role is highly execution-driven and performance-focused. You will work directly on the front line of our booking operations, responding to leads, guiding guests through the reservation process, and ensuring listings perform at a high level.

This is not a passive customer service role. Success in this position requires a strong sales mindset, fast response time, attention to detail, and the ability to actively follow up and close bookings.

You will work closely with cross-functional teams including operations, pricing, and marketing to continuously improve listing performance and maximize occupancy.



Required Qualifications



 Bachelor’s degree or above

 Experience in property management, short-term rental, hospitality, or related industry

 Fluent in English with strong written communication skills

 Strong sales mindset with ability to convert leads into bookings

 Ability to multitask and manage multiple inquiries simultaneously

 Strong sense of urgency and responsiveness

 Comfortable working in a fast-paced, performance-driven environment

 Basic proficiency with spreadsheets and digital tools



Preferred Qualifications

 2+ years of experience in short-term rental, OTA platforms, or sales roles

 Experience with Airbnb, VRBO, Zillow, or similar platforms

 Proven track record in lead conversion or sales performance

 Familiarity with PMS systems and booking workflows

 Ability to analyze listing performance and identify improvement opportunities

 Experience working with remote and cross-functional teams



***To proceed with the application process, please upload your resume into this Google form:
 and select the role.***

title: Remote Primary Care Nurse (RN)
description:
About Mulberry Health



Mulberry Health is a forward-thinking healthcare provider dedicated to delivering exceptional primary care services remotely. We are committed to leveraging technology to provide accessible, high-quality care to our patients. We are expanding our team and looking for three dedicated and skilled nurses from the Philippines to join us in our mission.



IMPORTANT — In-Person Training Requirement



Our CEO, Joseph Furtado, will be in Manila from April 12 to April 18, 2026. Candidates who are hired must be available to attend a mandatory in-person training session in Manila during that week. This is a paid training opportunity and a chance to meet our leadership team face-to-face before beginning your remote role. Please only apply if you can commit to being in Manila for this training period.



Job Summary



We are seeking experienced and compassionate Remote Primary Care Nurses to provide comprehensive virtual nursing care and back-office support. The ideal candidates will be responsible for a combination of clinical and administrative tasks, ensuring our patients receive the best possible care and our operations run smoothly. This is a full-time, remote position for candidates based in the Philippines.



Key Responsibilities



Primary Care Nursing (70%):

- Conduct virtual patient consultations via phone and video to assess patient conditions, provide nursing care, and offer patient education.

- Monitor and document patient vital signs, symptoms, and health progress in the EMR system.

- Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team of physicians, specialists, and other healthcare professionals to coordinate patient care.

- Develop and implement individualized patient care plans.

- Assist patients with medication management and adherence.



MA Back-Office Responsibilities (30%):

- Manage patient appointments, including scheduling, rescheduling, and sending reminders.

- Handle administrative tasks such as processing medical billing, managing patient records, and ensuring data accuracy.

- Transcribe physician's notes and update patient charts in the EMR system.

- Assist with prior authorizations and other insurance-related tasks.



Required Qualifications:

- Active and unrestricted Registered Nurse (RN) license in the Philippines (PRC License).

- Minimum of 2 years of clinical experience in a hospital or primary care setting.

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.

- Reliable high-speed internet connection (minimum 150 Mbps) and a quiet, professional home office setup.

- Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work independently.

- Must be available Mon-Fri, 9am-5pm Arizona time (MST/MDT).

- Must be available for in-person training in Manila from April 12-18, 2026.



Preferred Qualifications:

- Previous experience in a remote nursing or telehealth role.

- Proficiency with EMR/EHR systems, with a strong preference for eClinicalWorks (ECW). Experience with Epic, Athena, or similar platforms is also valued.

- US Registered Nurse (USRN) license is a strong advantage but not required.

- BLS and/or ACLS certifications are highly desirable.



Compensation and Benefits:

- Competitive hourly rate: $5.00 - $8.00 USD per hour (commensurate with experience)

- 15 days paid time off (PTO) annually

- 10 paid US holidays

- Opportunities for ongoing training and career advancement

title: Dispute Manager for DTC Men's Hair Brand
description:
Voke is a fast-growing direct-to-consumer men's hair wellness brand based in the US. We sell a natural DHT-blocking supplement (Pumpkin Seed Oil softgels) that helps men stop hair loss without the side effects of prescription drugs. We run lean, move fast, and operate fully remote via Slack.



We're looking for a Senior Dispute Manager to own our chargeback and payment dispute operations end-to-end. This is a full-time role with real ownership, you won't be filing tickets, you'll be building and running the process.



What You'll Own



Managing and resolving all chargebacks and payment disputes across our payment processors

Building and maintaining dispute response templates, evidence packages, and win-rate tracking

Monitoring chargeback ratios and flagging risk before it becomes a threshold issue

Working directly with our payment processors to represent disputes and protect revenue

Identifying patterns in dispute reasons and feeding insights back to the team (fulfillment, CS, ad claims)

Requirements



Proven experience managing payment disputes and chargebacks — this is non-negotiable

Deep hands-on experience with PayPal disputes (required)

Strong understanding of chargeback reason codes, representment strategy, and evidence documentation

Comfortable working async and remotely across time zones

Detail-oriented and proactive, you catch problems before they escalate

Bonus



Experience with Payarc is a strong plus

What to Expect



Full-time, remote position

All communication runs through Slack

You'll be working directly with a small, high-performance team

Fast feedback loops — decisions get made quickly here



To Apply, kindly fill-out the application form below:





Only those who fill-out the form will be screened for the position

title: Creative Strategist for DTC Brands / Dropshipping
description:
We’re Hiring a Creative Strategist (DTC / Dropshipping)



Flexible Schedule • Performance-Driven • Fast-Moving Creative Team



Hey Creative legends,



We’re an ambitious DTC e-commerce brand scaling aggressively across the UK and Germany. We’ve built past 8 figures without a dedicated Creative Strategist—but with Meta’s Andromeda update reshaping the game, it’s finally time to bring in someone who can own our creative direction.



If you know how to turn attention into conversions, break down angles and hooks, and build ads that actually perform, this role is for you.



This is not just a brief-writing job.

This is a strategic role that drives the entire direction of our creative output—concepts, scripts, research, testing, iteration, insights. If you love storytelling, performance ads, UGC structure, and analyzing what truly works, you’ll thrive here.



What You’ll Do



-Own creative strategy across UK & Germany

-Research hooks, angles, and trends using Meta Ads Library, TikTok, YouTube, and competitor analysis

-Build creative concepts and iterate based on real Meta performance data

- Master VSL copywriting, hooks, and story-driven ad frameworks

-Write clear, actionable creative briefs for editors, designers, and UGC creators

-Manage the creative testing pipeline:

Brainstorm ? Produce ? Test ? Analyze ? Iterate

-Lead and collaborate with a team of video editors

-Work closely with the Media Buyer to analyze CTR, CPC, CPA, CVR, and ROAS

-Use AI tools to speed up research, ideation, scripting, and testing

-Stay ahead of Meta trends (especially post-Andromeda)

-Organize creatives by angle, format, and funnel stage inside Notion / Google Drive



You’re a Great Fit If You:



-Have 2+ years in creative strategy, performance marketing, or DTC/dropshipping

-Understand direct-response psychology and why people click + buy

-Can explain why an ad works—and where the hook or angle comes from

-Are skilled in concept ideation, copywriting, and scripting

-Can manage creative teams and ensure fast, high-quality output

-Thrive in fast-paced, test-heavy environments with multiple product cycles

-Communicate clearly and professionally in English



Weekly Deliverables



-Creative briefs

-Hook & angle research

-VSL scripts

-Creative analysis with next-step recommendations

-Rapid testing and iteration (quality matters, but speed matters too)



???? Salary & Benefits



-$1,500 USD/month

-Performance bonuses tied to creative results

-Long-term role with strong growth potential

-Flexible working hours



???? How to Apply



Please submit your application using the ClickUp Form below:





Applications submitted outside the form will not be reviewed.

title: Media buyer
description:
Media buyer – Pressure Washing Marketing Agency







Part-time / Remote · 







About the role







We are a marketing agency that runs meta ads for pressure washing businesses across the US. We currently manage 14 clients and are growing. We are looking for a reliable media buyer to handle the day-to-day execution of our clients' ad campaigns.







You do NOT need to write ad copy, scripts, or strategy. We provide all the scripts .Your job is to edit the video creatives through the voiceovers we provide and footage we have and upload the creatives to the ad account of the client and launch the ads, monitor performance based on how many leads we are getting and report daily.







This is a flexible remote role. Workload varies day to day — some days are monitoring, others involve editing and launching. Estimated 5–8 hours per day on average across all clients







What you will do







Edit video ad creatives for each client using raw footage and scripts we provide (captions, cuts, music, branding)

Launch ads in Meta Ads Manager using the creatives you edited

Check each client's ads every day and monitor performance

Edit and update live campaigns based on instructions (audience, budget, copy)

Report back to the agency owner daily with a simple performance update

Flag any issues, disapprovals, or drops in performance immediately





Requirements







Proven hands-on experience with meta ads— must have managed at least $10,000 in total ad spend

Solid video editing skills — able to edit short-form video ads (captions, cuts, transitions, music)

Familiar with campaign structure: campaigns, ad sets, and ads

Knows how to set up audiences, budgets, placements, and objectives

Reliable, detail-oriented, and proactive in communication

Comfortable managing multiple client accounts simultaneously

Strong written English communication





Nice to have







Experience with home service or local business ads

Familiarity with CapCut

Familiarity with Go High Level (not required, we will train)





Pay



$100–$150 per client per month depending on performance and reliability. You will start with a small number of clients and grow as you prove yourself. Top performers managing 10+ clients can earn $1,000–$1,500/month and more 







How to apply







Application Link: 







(1) a short description of your meta ads experience and total ad spend managed.



(2) a short video on who you are and why you think you will be successful in this role.







Applicants who do not include all two will not be considered.

title: Full-Time 3D Hard Surface Artist — Sci-Fi & Military Vehicles (Game-Ready Assets)
description:
We are building a 3D asset business selling game-ready vehicle packs on platforms like Fab (Epic Games), CGTrader, and TurboSquid. We are looking for a skilled hard surface 3D artist to join full-time and produce sci-fi and military vehicles, props, and modular environment pieces.



You will receive detailed reference images and a production schedule for each asset pack. Your job is to model, UV unwrap, texture, rig (where needed), render, and export each model to strict quality standards. Every asset must be game-ready, PBR-compatible, and delivered in multiple file formats.

This is a long-term position. If you produce quality work on time, there is room for pay increases as revenue grows.



WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING

• Modeling hard surface vehicles, weapons, props, and environment kits from provided reference images

• Creating clean, optimized topology suitable for real-time game engines (UE5, Unity)

• UV unwrapping with efficient, non-overlapping layouts and consistent texel density

• Texturing with PBR Metallic-Roughness workflow in Substance Painter, Quixel Mixer, or equivalent

• Creating 3 LOD levels per model (LOD0 full detail, LOD1 50% reduction, LOD2 25%)

• Exporting to multiple formats: FBX, OBJ, BLEND (mandatory); MAX, Maya (if available)

• Producing all required renders: hero shots, multi-angle, wireframe overlays, UV layout screenshots, textured vs. untextured comparisons

• Creating 15-second turntable videos (MP4, 1080p) for each model

• Basic rigging and animation where specified (turret rotation, wheel spin, walk cycles, hatch open/close)

• Maintaining consistent design language, naming conventions, and folder structures across all packs



REQUIRED SKILLS

• 3+ years experience in hard surface 3D modeling (vehicles, weapons, mechanical objects)

• Strong topology skills — clean quads/tris, proper edge flow, no n-gons, manifold meshes

• PBR texturing experience — Substance Painter or Quixel Mixer (Metallic-Roughness workflow)

• UV unwrapping — manual layout, efficient use of 0-1 UV space, consistent texel density

• LOD creation — ability to produce optimized lower-detail versions that maintain silhouette quality

• Blender proficiency (primary tool) — modeling, UV, rendering (Cycles/EEVEE), export

• FBX/OBJ export experience — understanding of scale, transforms, and engine compatibility

• Rendering skills — studio lighting, clean presentation renders, turntable animations

• Basic rigging — simple mechanical rigs (rotating turrets, opening hatches, spinning wheels)



PREFERRED (NOT REQUIRED)

• Experience with Unreal Engine 5 (material setup, demo scenes)

• Experience with Unity HDRP

• Familiarity with 3ds Max or Maya

• Experience selling on Fab, CGTrader, TurboSquid, or similar marketplaces

• Concept art or design skills (ability to interpret loose references into detailed models)



SOFTWARE

• Required: Blender, Substance Painter (or Quixel Mixer)

• Preferred: 3ds Max, Maya, Marmoset Toolbag, ZBrush (for detail sculpting)

What We Provide

• Detailed reference images for every model

• A clear production schedule with weekly/monthly milestones

• Technical specifications document with exact quality standards

•  Creative direction and feedback on each deliverable

• Long-term, stable work with growth potential



TO APPLY

Please include in your application:

 1. Your portfolio — show hard surface work (vehicles, weapons, mechanical models). If you have marketplace listings, link them.

 2. Software you use — list your primary tools

 3. Your experience level — years of hard surface modeling, any marketplace/game studio experience

 4. Availability — can you start within 1-2 weeks? Can you commit full-time (40 hrs/week)?

 5. A short message about why you’re a good fit for this role

Do NOT apply if you primarily do character/organic modeling. This role is 100% hard surface.

title: Meta Ads Video Editor (With AI) For Ecommerce Ads
description:
Intro About the Company:

Hey there! I'm Kane, founder of a rapidly scaling 7-figure eCommerce brand in the women's shapewear & fashion space.

We've done over $2,000,000+ in revenue, driven almost entirely through Meta 
 & Instagram) ads, and we're now looking to bring on a high-level Video Editor who understands how to create winning ad creatives, not just "good-looking videos."



Your mission?



To turn scripts and concepts into scroll-stopping Meta ads using B-roll, UGC-style clips, AI tools, and proven ad-creative frameworks and eventually help ideate and write your own scripts based on concepts provided.





Job Qualifier Who This Is NOT For:

Important:



If you do NOT have direct experience editing ads for Shopify / eCommerce brands that run Meta ads, this role is not for you.

We're not looking for:

Long-form YouTube editors

Cinematic brand filmmakers

Beginners or generalist editors

Anyone without real ad results

But if you have edited Meta ads that actually convert, keep reading.

Why You Should Care to Apply

This brand is already proven.



We've:

Scaled to 7 figures using Meta ads

Validated product-market fit in a massive women's market

Built a repeatable creative testing system

Now, creative is the bottleneck.

That's where you come in.

You'll be working directly with the founder, not buried in layers of management, and you'll have real influence over what gets launched, tested, and scaled.

If you want your work to actually move revenue, this is it.



Who We're Looking For

We have one non-negotiable rule: You must want to get better and win.

The purpose of this role is to:

Create high-performing Meta ad creatives from provided scripts and concepts

Independently source clips, B-roll, and UGC-style footage

Edit, structure, and package ads optimized for Meta platforms

Eventually help write scripts and creative angles based on provided concepts

As a Video Editor, you have:

Proven experience editing Meta ads for eCommerce brands

A strong understanding of what performs on
 & Instagram

Experience with UGC-style ads, hooks, pacing, captions, and CTAs

Comfort using AI tools to speed up ideation, sourcing, or editing

Experience sourcing clips yourself (stock, UGC, B-roll, etc.)

You also have these character traits:

Strong attention to detail

Ownership mentality (you don't wait to be told every step)

Creative curiosity and testing mindset

Ability to think like a marketer, not just an editor



Your Day-to-Day Responsibilities

Edit short-form Meta ads (25 to 90 seconds) based on scripts provided

Source relevant B-roll, UGC-style clips, and visual assets independently

Structure ads for performance (hook, problem, solution, CTA)

Test different formats, pacing styles, hooks, and angles

Collaborate directly with the founder on creative concepts

Organize and deliver ad variations for testing

Eventually help write or improve scripts based on given concepts



More Reasons to Join

Work directly with a 7-figure eCommerce brand

High creative volume = fast improvement & leverage

Long-term opportunity with increased responsibility

Creative freedom within proven frameworks

Performance-based growth as the brand scales

Final Step

If this resonates with you, apply and include the number of the core value you align with most in your application.



CORE VALUES:

1 Results Obsessed

2 Attention to Detail

3 Accelerated Excellence

4 Relentless Ownership

5 Prepared to Win

6 Collaborative Innovation

7 Never Not Growing

Apply now. Let's build winning ads together.

title: Senior Holiday Homes Operations Manager
description:
Please only apply if you are serious and have relevant 5 years + experience. 



We are a fast-growing property management company specialising in long-term rentals and premium holiday homes across Auckland.



We are looking for a highly capable Holiday Homes Operations Manager to take ownership of our short-term rental portfolio and deliver an exceptional guest experience from enquiry through to checkout.



This is not a standard VA role — we are looking for someone experienced, proactive, and confident managing operations at a high level.



The Role



You will be responsible for the day-to-day management of our holiday home portfolio, acting as the central point of coordination between guests and our internal team.



Key responsibilities include:



-Managing all guest communication (pre-booking, during stay, and post-stay)

-Acting as the liaison between guests and our team (property manager, housekeeping, maintenance)

-Managing reservations, booking changes, payments, and add-ons (e.g. cots, extras)

-Preparing and onboarding new properties into our PMS (Guesty)

-Monitoring and maintaining a clear, organised inbox

-Triaging and responding to enquiries, and delegating where appropriate

-Supporting smooth day-to-day operations across the holiday home portfolio



About You



We are looking for someone who can take ownership and make decisions — not someone who needs constant direction.



You will ideally have:



-Strong experience in short-term rental or property management (Airbnb, Guesty or similar PMS)

title: Video Editor/Video Clipper
description:
Full-Time Video Editor / Video Clipper for Music Brand (Long-Term Remote Role)



Work directly with Fedarro

Job Type: Full-Time

Location: Remote

Industry: Music / Content / Education

Pay: Competitive monthly pay in USD

Payment: Weekly via Wise, Payoneer, or Remitly

Hours: Flexible, but deadlines and consistency matter

Growth Path: Long-term role with room for increased responsibility and compensation



About the Role

I’m looking for a high-level Video Editor / Video Clipper who wants a serious long-term opportunity, not just freelance gigs or random one-off projects.



You’ll be helping me turn raw content into powerful short-form and long-form videos that grow my audience, strengthen my brand, promote my offers, and support the bigger mission behind my business.



This is not a role for someone who only knows how to add captions and basic transitions.



I’m looking for someone who understands:



attention and retention

strong hooks

pacing and story flow

social-first editing

repurposing content across platforms

brand consistency

content volume without losing quality

If you are reliable, coachable, fast, organized, and want to grow with one brand over time, this could be a great fit.



About Me

Fedarro is a Grammy-nominated collaborating singer, songwriter, audio engineer, vocal producer, and music entrepreneur who helps music creatives make better music and turn their skills into income.



My content is built around music, creativity, coaching, artist development, mindset, studio sessions, and real-world music business insight. I’ve built multiple six-figure revenue streams in music and created a career outside the traditional industry model.



What You’ll Be Editing

You’ll edit content for:



TikTok

Instagram Reels

YouTube Shorts

YouTube long-form


Courses and programs

Main Responsibilities

Short-Form Content

You’ll turn raw footage into short-form clips such as:



educational talking-head clips

coaching clips

artist development content

studio session 
nts

behind-the-scenes footage

plugin and production breakdowns

product reviews

opinionated or controversial clips with strong hooks

Long-Form Content

You’ll also help edit:



YouTube videos

tutorials

studio breakdowns

course/program content

artist session recaps

equipment and production reviews

Expected Output

This role requires both speed and consistency.



You should be comfortable producing:



10–25 short-form videos per day

1–2 long-form videos per day, depending on footage and complexity

You do not need to overcomplicate edits. But you do need to know how to make content engaging, clean, and effective.



Who I’m Looking For

You are likely a strong fit if you:



have real experience editing TikTok, Reels, and Shorts

understand how to edit for retention, engagement, and replay value

can write or improve hooks, captions, and on-screen text

know how to identify strong 
nts in long footage

can edit fast without constantly sacrificing quality

communicate clearly in English

take feedback well and improve quickly

can stay organized with files, revisions, and delivery

are dependable and don’t disappear

want a stable, long-term remote role

can follow direction while also thinking creatively

are comfortable using CapCut, Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar tools

can repurpose content across multiple platforms

can schedule posts if needed

This Role Is NOT for You If

Please do not apply if:



you are an agency

you manage a team and won’t be doing the editing yourself

you are looking for side work only

you are applying to hundreds of jobs with copy-paste messages

you need constant chasing to meet deadlines

you are not comfortable with high-volume editing

you are a beginner with no real short-form editing experience

you disappear when feedback gets direct

you want creative freedom but don’t want structure, consistency, or standards

I want someone professional, mature, and serious about doing great work over the long term.



What Matters Most in This Role

I care a lot about:



reliability

speed

communication

consistency

coachability

taste

attention to detail

long-term fit

If you’re amazing but inconsistent, this will not work.



If you’re strong, disciplined, and committed, this can become a major long-term role.



Content Style References

Please review these examples to understand the type of content and editing direction that performs well for my brand:



Instagram / TikTok References



No Longer Registering Songs (Controversy)

You Have to Be Patient Working with Artist

You Have to Be Patient w/ Dope Singers

Coachable Artist Always Win

How I Coached This Artist & Made a Crazy Hook

Your Song Should Be A Conversation

Teaching One Of My Student How to Write a Hook

I Made A Template By Engineering Rlyo

Editing Direction / SOP Playlist



YouTube Playlist Reference

Application Process

I only want applicants who actually read the post and can follow directions.



Step 1: Send an
 to





Step 2: Use this subject line

OLJ Video Editor Application – [Your Name]



Step 3: Include ALL of the following in your

Your full name

Your OnlineJobs.ph profile link

Your portfolio or best 3–5 editing samples

A short note explaining:

how long you’ve been editing

what content niches you’ve worked in

what editing software you use

Your weekly availability

Your internet speed

Your computer specs

Your expected monthly salary in USD

A short answer to this question:

Why do you think you’re a good fit for this role?

Step 4: Add this line at the top of your

“Long-term, reliable, and ready to grow.”



If that line is missing, I will assume you did not read the post carefully.



Test Task

Shortlisted applicants will be asked to complete a test edit based on my content style.



I prefer to review your portfolio and application quality first before moving forward, so only serious candidates will receive the next step.



This helps save time for both sides and keeps the process focused on people who are genuinely a fit.



What You Can Expect If Hired

If you’re the right fit, this can become a long-term role with:



steady work

clear direction

room to improve your skillset

increasing trust and responsibility

long-term growth with one brand instead of constantly chasing new clients

I value loyalty, consistency, and people who take pride in doing strong work.



If that sounds like you, apply.



Optional stronger version for even better filtering

If you want, you can add this line near the top of the job post:



Preference will be given to applicants who have experience editing for coaches, personal brands, educators, music creators, or social-first content brands.



That will reduce generic applicants even more.



My honest recommendation

For higher-quality filtering, this version is better than asking everyone to do the test immediately.



Why:



serious applicants will complete the detailed application properly

low-quality applicants usually skip instructions

you won’t get buried under random test submissions from people who aren’t a fit

you can shortlist only the best candidates for the edit test

title: MOBILE IOS SOFTWARE ENGINEER
description:
About Orani                                                                   

                                                                                

  Orani is an AI-powered virtual receptionist built for solo and micro service

  businesses — cleaners, contractors, realtors, detailers — who lose revenue    

  every time a call goes unanswered. Orani gives every independent operator a

  dedicated business number backed by an AI that answers calls, captures caller 

  intent, and delivers structured summaries and follow-up actions directly to

  the owner's phone.



  We're a small, fast-moving team building a product that genuinely helps people

   run their businesses better.





  The Role

          

  You'll be working across the full product — mobile UI, backend API, and

  telephony logic. Most of what you'll touch is already built; your job is to   

  fix bugs, ship new features, and raise the overall quality bar. You should be

  comfortable jumping between a Flutter screen and a Django webhook in the same 

  afternoon.                                                



  ---

  What You'll Work On

                                                                                

  - Building and refining screens in Flutter across iOS and Android

  - Maintaining and extending the Django backend — API endpoints, business      

  logic, database models                                    

  - Working with Twilio webhooks and TwiML to control call routing behavior

  - Integrating with VAPI for AI voice assistant configuration and call handling

  - Fixing real user-reported issues — onboarding flows, form validation,       

  settings UX

  - Keeping the EC2 deployment stable and shipping updates without breaking     

  production                                                





  You're a Strong Fit If You

                                                                                

  - Have shipped a Flutter app before — you know Dart, widget trees, and state

  management doesn't intimidate you                                             

  - Have built or maintained a Django backend — models, views, REST APIs, ORM

  queries

  - Have worked with at least one telephony or communication API (Twilio,

  Vonage, Bandwidth)                                                            

  - Are comfortable reading and writing TwiML or similar call flow markup

  - Can SSH into an EC2 instance, debug a running service, and deploy a fix     

  without hand-holding                                      

  - Move fast, write clean code, and communicate clearly about what's done and

  what's blocked                                                                

   

  ---                                                                           

  Nice to Have                                              



  - Experience with VAPI or other AI voice platforms

  - Familiarity with prompt engineering or LLM integrations

  - Prior work on a consumer mobile app with real users



  ---

  What We're Not Looking For

                            

  Someone who needs a fully specc'd ticket for every task. This codebase is

  real, the bugs are real, and the users are real. You should be able to look at

   a problem, understand the system, and figure out the fix.

                                                                                

  ---                                                       

  Stack: Flutter · Dart · Python · Django · Twilio · VAPI · TwiML · AWS EC2

                                                                                



  To Apply                                                                      

                                                            

  Send a short note about something you've built — mobile, backend, or both —

  and why this kind of product interests you. Links to work are more useful than

   a resume.

title: Real Estate Lead Generation Specialist (Cold Calling + GoHighLevel CRM)
description:
We are a U.S.-based real estate investment group looking for a top-tier cold caller who can consistently turn outbound calls into qualified seller leads and booked appointments. This will be long term position. 



This role is for experienced professionals only. If you are used to reading scripts without understanding deals, this is not a fit.

We are building a serious acquisition pipeline, and your performance directly impacts revenue. Salary increases and bonuses based on performance are available for the right candidate. 



What You Will Do

1. High-Volume Calling

• Call 120–250+ leads daily

• Speak with U.S. property owners (clear, professional English required)

• Handle objections confidently (price, timing, “not interested”)

2. Seller Qualification

You must identify:

• Motivation (why they would sell)

• Timeline (urgent vs long-term)

• Property condition

• Price expectations

3. Appointment Setting

• Book qualified appointments directly to our acquisition team

• Ensure high show rates through proper confirmation

4. GoHighLevel (GHL) CRM Management

• Update pipeline stages accurately

• Add detailed notes after every call

• Trigger SMS/email follow-ups using GHL workflows

• Maintain clean data (this is critical)

5. Follow-Up

• Re-contact warm leads

• Convert “not now” into future opportunities

• Use calls, SMS, and voi
 strategically

 

Requirements (Strict)

• Minimum 2 years real estate cold calling (U.S. market)

• Strong English communication (neutral accent preferred)



• Experience with:

o GoHighLevel (required)

o CRM pipelines + lead tracking

• Stable internet + quiet workspace

• Ability to work U.S. business hours

 

Compensation

• Competitive hourly base (based on experience)

• Top performers can significantly increase income through bonuses

 

KPIs (How You Are Measured)

Metric Target

Calls/day 120–250

Conversations/day 15–40

Qualified leads/week 10–25

Appointments/week 5–15

 

Who This Role Is For

• You have worked with U.S. sellers before

• You understand real estate terms (ARV, comps, condition, motivation)

• You are confident handling rejection

• You care about performance, not just hours

 

How to Apply (Required)

To be considered, you must complete the 10-minute screening test below.

Applications without this will NOT be reviewed.

 

5-Minute Screening Test 

Part 1: Quick Deal Understanding

You call a homeowner. They say:

“I might sell… the house needs some work. Zillow says $350,000 but I’d probably take around $300,000.”

Additional info:

• Repairs needed: ~$40,000

• ARV (after repair value): ~$360,000

Question:

1. Is this a potentially good deal? Why or why not?

2. What is the maximum offer range you would aim for (ballpark)?

3. What would you say next to move the conversation forward?

 

Part 2: Objection Handling 

Seller says:

“I’m not in a rush. I’ll just list it with an agent.”

Question:

Write your exact response (2–4 sentences max).

We are looking for:

• Confidence

• Reframing (not arguing)

• Ability to reopen the conversation

 

Part 3: GoHighLevel Execution 

Explain briefly:

1. How do you move a lead through a pipeline in GoHighLevel?

2. What actions do you take immediately after a call?

 

Part 4: Audio Submission

Record a 60–90 second sample call opening where you:

• Introduce yourself

• Engage the seller

• Ask your first qualification question

 

What We Are Looking For in Your Answers

We are NOT looking for perfect answers—we are looking for:

• Real understanding of deal logic

• Ability to think, not just read scripts

• Strong communication and confidence

• Familiarity with CRM workflows

 

Final Note

We hire long-term operators, not short-term callers.

If you perform, there is opportunity for growth.  This role can evolve into:

• Lead manager

• Acquisition support

• Team lead

title: Instagram Setter (DM Outreach + Appointment Setting)
description:
We're looking for a reliable Instagram Setter to handle outbound DM outreach for our agency. You'll be working directly inside our Instagram account – no content creation, no calls, just focused outreach and appointment setting.



Your daily tasks:



- Find 20 qualified leads per day matching our ICP (we provide the profile criteria)

- Send outreach DMs using our proven script (provided + will be explained)

- Respond to replies – use a translator if needed, keep it natural

- Qualify leads and book calls directly into our calendar

- Log all conversations and outcomes in a simple tracker



Once a lead shows genuine interest (reply stage 2), you'll build a personalized Miro board for them using our provided template. No creativity needed — you follow the template, fill in the lead's details, and send the board link in the DMs as a free value asset before the call.



This is our lead magnet and a key part of the process. Boards should be completed within a few hours of the lead reaching reply stage 2.



What this requires:



- Basic Miro skills (we provide the template + walkthrough)

- Attention to detail when filling in lead-specific info

- Fast turnaround – the board goes out while the lead is still warm



This is straightforward work if you're organized and consistent. We don't need someone creative – we need someone reliable who shows up every day and follows the process.



What we offer:



- Fixed daily rate of $5/day (~$150/month base)

- 4% commission on every closed deal that comes from your booked appointments

- Our average client deal is $2.500-4.500 – your commission per closed deal: $
Close 2-3 deals/month through your appointments and you're making $200-350+ on top of base

- Long-term position for the right person



Requirements:



- Active Instagram user, comfortable navigating DMs

- Basic English reading/writing (translator tools allowed)

- Organized – you'll track your own outreach daily

- Available throughout the day to reply within 1 hour when leads respond

- Total daily time commitment is roughly 1-2 hours, flexible across the day

Strong organizational and time management skills

Excellent communication skills (written and verbal)

A team player with a positive attitude

Why Join Us:



Supportive and team-oriented work environment

Opportunity to grow within the company

Be part of a team that works hand-in-hand to achieve success



If you’re someone who is proactive, dependable, and eager to contribute to a team’s success, we’d love to hear from you!

title: Motion Graphics Editor Needed for 60–90 Second Fintech Onboarding Explainer Video (Storyboard Provided)
description:
I am building a short onboarding explainer video (60–90 seconds) for a financial platform called inara.



This video will be used inside our onboarding flow and also shared with institutional partners, pilot organizations, and program leaders. The goal is to clearly explain how structured access to capital works through our system.



The storyboard and scene structure are already defined. Some visual direction and references are already prepared.



This is not a concept development project.



This is an execution project for someone who can assemble and animate a clean institutional-quality explainer video using an existing structure.



We are looking for someone who can help establish a consistent motion style that we can continue using across future product and partner videos.



?



What the Video Explains



The video walks viewers through:



• why instability often happens because access to capital is unpredictable

• how structured contribution groups work

• how payout timing becomes predictable

• how predictability improves financial decision-making

• how this differs from traditional reactive financial tools

• closing positioning message about structured access to capital



Target video length:



60–90 seconds



?



Style Direction



The animation style should feel:



clean

calm

structured

modern

institutional



Similar pacing and tone to:



Stripe product explainers

Apple Wallet onboarding motion

modern fintech infrastructure visuals



Not cartoon animation

Not influencer-style editing

Not marketing hype animation



?



Assets Provided



You will receive:



scene-by-scene storyboard

script structure

existing visual references

color palette

text content

layout direction



The structure of the video is already planned. I am looking for execution quality rather than concept creation.



?



Deliverables



You will:



animate scenes based on storyboard

create smooth transitions between scenes

highlight payout timing clearly inside the animation

match typography across scenes

match color palette across scenes

maintain consistent motion pacing

assemble the full onboarding video

prepare export-ready final video (60–90 seconds)



?



Software



After Effects preferred



Also acceptable:



Premiere

Figma animation export

Lottie workflows

Canva Pro motion export

Luma-assisted animation workflows



Use whichever tools you are strongest with as long as the final result matches institutional-quality explainer motion.



?



Timeline



Expected turnaround:



2–4 days



?



Budget



This project budget is $200 for the first onboarding explainer video.



We are building a long-term product and expect to produce additional motion assets including:



product onboarding updates

partner-facing explainer videos

website motion sections

investor demo visuals

institutional presentation assets



Strong performance on this first project will lead to ongoing paid motion work.



We are looking for someone interested in helping build a consistent animation style with us over time.



?



To Apply



Please include:



1 example explainer video you created

1 example motion graphics animation

which software you plan to use

your estimated delivery timeline



Also confirm whether you are interested in ongoing motion graphics work after this first project.

title: Media Buyer (Meta Ads) – Remote
description:
Media Buyer (Meta Ads) – Remote



We are looking for an experienced Media Buyer to manage Meta ad campaigns across multiple client accounts.



This role is not for beginners. You will be responsible for performance, communication, and consistency across a portfolio of clients in the home improvement space.



---



What You’ll Be Doing



Managing and optimising Meta 
 & Instagram) ad campaigns

Handling multiple client accounts at once

Monitoring performance and making data-driven decisions daily

Working closely with our internal team (CSMs, SDRs, creatives)

Ensuring campaigns are delivering consistent lead flow and results



---



Requirements



Minimum $250,000 in ad spend managed (must be proven)

At least 4+ years of experience running Meta ads

Experience working with local service businesses

Strong preference for experience in home improvement (bathrooms, kitchens, windows, roofing, etc.)

Previous agency experience (used to managing multiple clients simultaneously)

Strong communication skills – must speak fluent English

Ability to work within a team and communicate clearly



If you haven’t worked in an agency before, your results and experience must be exceptionally strong.



---



Working Hours



8:00 AM – 6:00 PM (UK time)



---



Compensation



$2,000/month base salary

Performance bonus: $500 – $1,000/month



Bonuses are tied directly to performance.

If you are not hitting the minimum performance standard, this role is not for you.



---



Important



This is a full-time role.

If you take this position, you must not be working with any other clients or agencies.



We are looking for someone fully committed who will work exclusively with us.



---



Who This Is For



Someone **hungry and driven

Someone who takes ownership and responsibility

Someone who understands that results matter

Someone who is comfortable working in a fast-paced agency environment



---



Who This Is NOT For



* Beginners or junior media buyers

* Freelancers juggling multiple unrelated projects

* Anyone who cannot handle multiple clients or team communication



---



How to Apply



To apply, you **must send a Loom video (1–2 minutes)** covering:



* Your experience

* Ad spend managed

* Industries you’ve worked in

* Case studies or results



Applications without a Loom video will not be considered.

title: Product Research, SEO Listings and  Customer Support on Vinted and Etsy ($180 per month)
description:
Do you want to work on a cool fashion and e-commerce project from home, with complete autonomy?

We are looking for a creative, resourceful, and curious person to help us find products and create perfect visuals for our Vinted store using AI.



Your (daily) missions:



Use Gemini AI to research trending fashion niches using our prompts.



Get inspired via Pinterest to understand the visual universe of these niches.



Find similar items on platforms like AliExpress, Temu, and Shein.



Generate realistic and stylish photos according to our methods (we will train you on this) using Gemini AI and ChatGPT.



What we expect from you:



To be autonomous and constantly improving.



To enjoy brainstorming, getting inspired, and immersing yourself in new niches to find the most suitable products.



To be meticulous regarding the quality of the generated photos.



No need to be a design expert: if you know how to use Gemini and have a fashion view on Shein, you already have a big advantage.



Compensation:



$180 per month (7 hours a day).



Paid time off.



Opportunities for growth and evolution.



Conditions:



100% Remote work.



Flexible working hours.



Complete training with ready-to-use processes upon your arrival.



How to apply:

Send a short message explaining:

(Send a cover letter through the platform)



Hello and welcome! My name is Kyoungbu Kang. This is my TikTok channel to understand what I do: 



To move forward, I'd like to invite you to a short test.



This task is designed to test your understanding, your ability to follow instructions, and how well you interpret style and detail — not just basic execution.



Here's the full step-by-step guide for the task (Google Doc):





Here's the video where I explain everything visually:





Important Notes:

– Don't worry if it's not perfect.

– What matters most is that you do your best, follow each step carefully, and show you understand what I'm really looking for.

– If you go a little further or improve a small part: that's a big plus



 About Gemini:

The image generation step requires Gemini with image access (ideally Gemini Pro).

If you don't have Pro, try with the free version — it usually works, but with limitations.

Please let me know right away if you can't generate images, so I can find a solution.



Once you've completed the task, please send everything in one
 or message.



Send me your test by
 (you can use a Google Drive link) and also include your WhatsApp number.



Thanks again, and if your work is perfect for me, we will do a call together to explain what products I'm selling right now, and how to respond and list products on Vinted and Etsy.



There is also a possibility for evolution in salary to $300 per month for a job as a manager if you're good at your job.

title: Creative Strategist - US Market
description:
Fill out form: (ADD PORFOLIO IN FORM)





About Us



We operate a portfolio of performance-led DTC brands across the US and Southeast Asian markets. Our brands are in the 7–8 figure range, and our growth is built entirely on creative research, testing, iteration, and the discipline to do all three at volume.



We run a structured, data-driven creative system. Testing velocity and iteration discipline are what determine how fast we grow. If you want to work somewhere that treats creative strategy as a real performance function, not a vibes-based exercise, this is it.



As a US Creative Strategist, you'll be dedicated to our US market brands, developing and testing concepts for American audiences with the specific buyer psychology, market awareness stages, and offer positioning that move cold traffic in that market.

 

Compensation

This role offers a competitive base salary plus performance-based commissions tied directly to winning concepts that validate and scale. You earn more when your creative drives results - it's that simple.

 

What You'll Be Doing



Research & Concept Development

•   	Developing 15–20 net-new concepts per week across video and image formats. Typically, video concepts make up 70–80% of your weekly output, with image concepts making up the balance; this ratio shifts based on what's scaling and where the priorities are

•   	Conducting deep research into high-impact angles, unique mechanisms, and market awareness stages to develop breakthrough concepts for US audiences

•   	Studying competitive intelligence (ad spying), consumer psychology, mass desire, and internal winners to identify untapped opportunities specific to the US market

•   	Building concepts that are rooted in how American buyers think, what they respond to, and what's already saturated in the market



Scriptwriting & Creative Execution

•   	Writing compelling, high-converting direct-response scripts designed to hook cold US audiences and drive them to purchase (and potentially work in tandem with a dedicated copywriter)

•   	Producing clear, detailed briefs for editors and designers - tight enough that the execution team can move fast without ambiguity

•   	Owning the creative pipeline for your assigned brands: research ? ideation ? scripting ? post-launch performance analysis and iteration

•   	Writing across formats: UGC-style scripts, direct-address video ads, static ad copy, and image concept briefs with the same conversion logic applied to each



Collaboration & Iteration

•   	Working closely with the Lead Strategist at the strategic level and with editors and designers at the execution level

•   	Leveraging proven copy structures, frameworks, and past winners to accelerate testing velocity

•   	Feeding insights back to the wider team to continuously improve our creative playbook



Performance Analysis & Scaling

•   	Analyzing winners and losers weekly - documenting what worked, why it worked, and what to test next

•   	Rapidly iterating winning concepts into multiple variations to validate and scale

 

What We're Looking For



Must-Haves

•   	Proven direct-response experience > you've written scripts, hooks, or ad concepts that have run on Meta or TikTok (paid socials)

•   	Strong copywriting skills across video and static formats > your writing pulls people in and keeps them moving toward a purchase

•   	Deep understanding of consumer psychology, awareness stages, and what makes cold traffic convert in the  target market

•   	Obsessive researcher > you go beyond surface-level competitive intel and find angles other people miss

•   	High output capacity, averaging 15–20 concepts a week, is the expectation, not the ceiling, and you can sustain it without the quality slipping



Instant No's

•   	You don't have real experience in ad creative strategy or direct-response copywriting

•   	You want to spend weeks polishing one campaign > this role requires speed, iteration, and high-quality output simultaneously

•   	You struggle with volume, operational workflows, or fast feedback cycles

•   	You're uncomfortable with the reality that most ads fail before one wins > failure is data, not a problem

•   	You're balancing multiple jobs or clients > this requires full-time, dedicated ownership

 

What Success Looks Like

•   	Consistently producing 15–20 net-new concepts per week across video and image formats

•   	Achieving a 10%+ creative hit rate -concepts that validate, scale, and drive real spend

•   	Operating as a reliable creative partner to the editors, designers, and lead strategists around you

 

Why Join Us

•   	Hands-on experience working on 7–8 figure brands with real paid media budgets behind them

•   	Performance-based upside - base + commissions tied directly to winners and scaling impact

•   	High ownership - your concepts directly influence spend, revenue, and growth
